authors: De Carolis G, Paroli M, Tollapi L, Doust M, Burgher AH, Yu C, Yang T, Morgan D, Amirdelfan K, Kapural L, Sitzman BT, Bundschu R, Vallejo R, Benyamin R, Yearwood TL, Gliner BE, Powell AA, Bradley K
title: Paresthesia-Independence: An Assessment of Technical Factors Related to 10 kHz Paresthesia-Free Spinal Cord Stimulation
study_question: Is HF10 therapy pain relief dependent on paresthesia-pain overlap or physiologic midline positioning?
population_assessed: 61 patients with chronic intractable back and leg pain implanted with HF10 therapy device for up to 24 months.
outcome_measures: Pain relief percentage, paresthesia-pain overlap percentage, paresthesia laterality, mediolateral distribution
inclusion_criteria: Patients permanently implanted with Senza system receiving HF10 therapy for chronic intractable low back and/or leg pain.
answer_to_study_question: No correlation between paresthesia-pain overlap and HF10 efficacy. Mediolateral positioning not statistically significant predictor of pain relief.
